hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 7, A. The table saw a raise, which both you and your opponent called, setting the stage for a potentially explosive hand.
Flop — 7, 6, 9. You decided to bet, but your opponent swiftly raised, indicating they might have found something they liked in the community cards.
Turn — 8. Your opponent took the initiative with a bet, and you chose to call, sensing the potential for a bigger pot but not wanting to commit too much just yet.
River — 10. With the final card in place, your opponent fired again — half the pot, putting significant pressure on your decision-making process.

wrongReasons: 9 2 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 9 2.
J 10 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with J 10.
8 K has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 8 K.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 10

Your opponent's hand improved significantly on the turn with the arrival of the 8, making a straight. Their consistent betting pattern, especially the aggressive bets on the turn and river, suggests they were value betting with a made hand, which aligns perfectly with holding A 10.
